title: Repository and working tree deterministic misconception check

publication.changeSummary: Deterministic misconception item for Repository and working tree; blocked from formal exams until independent human review.

items.title: Repository and working tree: correct the failure model
items.prompt: Which statement correctly resolves this documented beginner mistake? The repository is just the visible project folder, so every file in it is automatically versioned.

examEligibility.rationale: The deterministic answer and rubric are AI-assisted draft content awaiting independent human approval.

hints: Compare both options with the canonical model, declared boundary, and observable evidence in the lesson.

feedback.correct: Correct. Distinguish Git's object/reference database from the working tree and use status to see which visible files are tracked, modified, staged, or untracked.
feedback.incorrect: The selected statement preserves the documented misconception. Distinguish Git's object/reference database from the working tree and use status to see which visible files are tracked, modified, staged, or untracked.

criteria.description: Selects the source-aligned correction for Repository and working tree instead of the documented misconception.

privateAuthorNotes: Human reviewer must verify the correction against the cited source and ensure the misconception option is plausible without reinforcing it.

options.text: Distinguish Git's object/reference database from the working tree and use status to see which visible files are tracked, modified, staged, or untracked.
The repository is just the visible project folder, so every file in it is automatically versioned.
